disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recurrent bruising on the inner upper arms and torso, attributed by the patient to a minor fall from a low bed; the account is vague and shifting and some marks look several days old.
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me how the bruises happened?
When did you notice these marks?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I fell out of bed, I'm just clumsy. I don't really remember. Can I please just have something for the pain.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: It's nothing, it's just my age, I bruise easily.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The accompanying family member interrupts, answers over the patient, is controlling and hostile, and is pressing hard for strong (opioid) analgesia; the patient becomes quiet, withdrawn and fearful when the family member speaks.
trigger_question_examples: Can I hear from you in your own words how you've been?
Who helps you at home, and how are things going?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: [The family member cuts in] She's fine, she just fell, she needs proper pain killers, not questions. [The patient looks down and says little.]

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Signs of neglect in a fully dependent bedbound patient — poor hygiene, low weight / poor intake, a developing sacral pressure sore, no water or call bell in reach, and a strong smell of urine.
trigger_question_examples: Are you able to eat and drink enough?
How is your skin — any sore areas from lying in bed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I don't get up much. I'm not really hungry. My back is sore where I lie. I can't reach the water most of the time.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Dehydration — very dry mouth and lips, poor fluid intake, in a frail elder who has also stopped her antihypertensive.
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My mouth is really dry. I haven't had much to drink today.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient will only hint at being frightened, and only when the family member is not within earshot — she asks not to make trouble.
trigger_question_examples: Is it alright if we speak just the two of us for a moment — do you feel safe at home?
Is there anything you'd like to tell me privately?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: [Quietly, when alone] Please don't say anything. I don't want any trouble. I just want to be left in peace.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: [If the family member is present] Everything's fine. They look after me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Significant delay in seeking care for obvious injuries, and the patient's regular medications have lapsed because the family says money has suddenly become very tight with unpaid bills.
trigger_question_examples: How long have these marks been there before today?
Have there been any problems getting her medications or paying for things?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: [Family member] We couldn't get in earlier, and money's been tight, so she's been off her tablets a while.

deflection_behaviours.description: The family member repeatedly redirects the consultation to demanding strong pain medication and blocks any attempt to hear the patient's own account or to speak with her privately.
deflection_behaviours.trigger_condition: Whenever the clinician asks the patient to describe events herself or requests a private word.
deflection_behaviours.deflection_text_template: She doesn't need all these questions, she needs painkillers. Just prescribe something strong and we'll manage at home.
